medical_history.chief_complaint: fevers, night sweats and non-productive cough
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: 2-month history of fevers, night sweats and non-productive cough. He had been on a series of consecutive trials of daclizumab for Relapsing Remitting Multiple Sclerosis (RRMS), totalling 79 months (12-months placebo-controlled, 12-months dose-blinded, 55-months open-label of 150 mg monthly subcutaneously). He had experienced three relapses in the three years prior to commencing the trials but remained relapse-free during them.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological examination demonstrated longstanding altered left-sided facial sensation, mild instability on Romberg testing; Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) score was stable at 2.0.

radiographic_examinations.findings: High avidity lymphadenopathy in the mediastinum and abdominal chains, but no visceral uptake
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: 6-months following daclizumab cessation demonstrated reduction in lymph node uptake

radiographic_examinations.findings: Stable with no new or enhancing lesions and no meningeal enhancement

other_tests.findings: FEV1 2.13 (62.3% predicted), FVC 3.62 (83.7% predicted), FEV1/FVC ratio 58.8% (moderate obstruction)
other_tests.changes_over_time: Repeat spirometry 10-weeks following discontinuation of corticosteroids was normal; FEV1 3.01 (88% predicted), FVC 3.74 (86.5% predicted), FEV1/FVC ratio 80.5%

other_tests.procedure_name: Lymph node aspirate via endobronchial ultrasound
